title: Q2 renewal forecast lands within 2% of committed ARR
anchor: Forecast called $1.8M, actual closed at $1.77M, variance at 1.7%.
body: Forecast called $1.8M, actual closed at $1.77M, variance at 1.7%. Trailing four quarters averaged 8.2% variance.
trace: Q2 forecast accuracy significantly improved versus trailing pattern, suggesting renewal pipeline visibility has strengthened.

title: At-risk ARR pool contracts by $90K month over month
anchor: April pool sits at $220K across 3 accounts versus March's $310K across 7 accounts.
connect: Top-20 ARR concentration in the at-risk pool dropped from 5 accounts to 2.
body: April pool sits at $220K across 3 accounts versus March's $310K across 7 accounts. Top-20 ARR concentration in the at-risk pool dropped from 5 accounts to 2.
trace: At-risk pool shrinkage concentrated in top-ARR accounts, which typically carry renewal execution complexity.

title: Mid-market NRR jumps 7 points above trailing three-quarter average
anchor: Q2 mid-market NRR sits at 112% versus the 104.8% three-quarter average.
connect: Mid-market customers running Atlas Insights expanded at 75% rate during Q2.
body: Q2 mid-market NRR sits at 112% versus the 104.8% three-quarter average. Mid-market customers running Atlas Insights expanded at 75% rate during Q2.
trace: Mid-market NRR acceleration correlates with Atlas Insights adoption driving expansion behavior.

title: Multi-product customers compound NRR at 8-point premium over single-product
anchor: Multi-product NRR runs at 124% in Q2 versus 102% for single-product customers.
connect: Multi-product share of the customer base sits at 38.3%.
body: Multi-product NRR runs at 124% in Q2 versus 102% for single-product customers. Multi-product share of the customer base sits at 38.3%.
trace: Product breadth drives material NRR lift, with multi-product customers representing minority of base but majority of expansion behavior.

title: Time to first value compresses 15 days in Q2 mid-market cohort
anchor: Q2 mid-market TTFV runs at 23 days versus Q1's 38 days across 24 accounts.
connect: Q1 mid-market cohort retention correlates with TTFV: under 30 days 91%, over 60 days 74%.
body: Q2 mid-market TTFV runs at 23 days versus Q1's 38 days across 24 accounts. Q1 mid-market cohort retention correlates with TTFV: under 30 days 91%, over 60 days 74%.
trace: TTFV acceleration in Q2 positions the cohort for stronger retention based on Q1 correlation patterns.

title: Coverage tier retention divergence widens to 14 points in Q2
anchor: High-touch GRR sits at 96% versus tech-touch at 82%, 14-point spread.
connect: High-touch covers 18.3% of the customer base weighted to top ARR.
body: High-touch GRR sits at 96% versus tech-touch at 82%, 14-point spread. High-touch covers 18.3% of the customer base weighted to top ARR.
trace: Coverage model creates material retention variance, with high-touch tier protecting top ARR accounts at premium retention rate.

title: Customer health review expansion sourcing outpaces outbound by 2:1 acceptance
anchor: March health-review-sourced opportunities: sales accepted 5 of 6 at 83% rate.
connect: Outbound expansion opportunities: sales accepted 7 of 17 at 41% rate.
body: March health-review-sourced opportunities: sales accepted 5 of 6 at 83% rate. Outbound expansion opportunities: sales accepted 7 of 17 at 41% rate.
trace: Health review expansion sourcing generates higher-quality opportunities with double the sales acceptance rate versus outbound motion.

title: License utilization above 80% grows by 8 accounts in mid-market
anchor: Q2 mid-market customers above 80% utilization: 22 versus Q1's 14.
connect: Multi-product customers expanded ARR at 75% rate during Q2.
body: Q2 mid-market customers above 80% utilization: 22 versus Q1's 14. Multi-product customers expanded ARR at 75% rate during Q2.
trace: License utilization climb in mid-market correlates with quarter's expansion acceleration, signaling usage-driven upgrade behavior.

title: Mid-market health distribution shifts 7 points toward green in Q2
anchor: Q2 mid-market green health: 78% versus Q1's 71% across 55 accounts.
connect: Q1 green-health customers renewed at 95% rate versus yellow-health at 75%.
body: Q2 mid-market green health: 78% versus Q1's 71% across 55 accounts. Q1 green-health customers renewed at 95% rate versus yellow-health at 75%.
trace: Health distribution improvement in Q2 positions mid-market for stronger renewal performance based on Q1 health-to-retention correlation.

title: Beacon Logistics renews three months early on $280K ARR
anchor: Renewal signed April 21 versus July 15 original contract end date.
connect: Custom Permissions and Audit Logs ships June 15, ahead of original renewal window.
body: Renewal signed April 21 versus July 15 original contract end date. Custom Permissions and Audit Logs ships June 15, ahead of original renewal window.
trace: Early renewal timing aligns with product launch calendar, suggesting launch readiness influenced renewal acceleration.

title: Enterprise tier retention leads segments at 94% GRR despite expansion lag
anchor: Q2 enterprise GRR sits at 94% with NRR at 106% across $432K renewing book.
connect: Mid-market NRR runs at 112% with small-business at 102%.
body: Q2 enterprise GRR sits at 94% with NRR at 106% across $432K renewing book. Mid-market NRR runs at 112% with small-business at 102%.
trace: Enterprise segment shows strong retention but lags expansion versus mid-market, suggesting different growth patterns by tier.

title: Q1 all-segments cohort 90-day retention climbs 5 points to 88%
anchor: Q1 cohort 90-day retention at 88% across 60 accounts versus Q4's 83% across 55 accounts.
connect: Q1 cohort TTFV compressed to 26 days versus Q4's 38 days.
body: Q1 cohort 90-day retention at 88% across 60 accounts versus Q4's 83% across 55 accounts. Q1 cohort TTFV compressed to 26 days versus Q4's 38 days.
trace: Early-stage retention improvement correlates with TTFV compression, suggesting onboarding acceleration compounds into retention lift.

title: Executive sponsor depth strengthens in 3 of 9 April enterprise renewals
anchor: April enterprise renewals signed: 9, with 3 showing executive sponsor depth increase during Q1.
connect: Top-20 at-risk accounts with executive sponsor review scheduled this week: 2.
body: April enterprise renewals signed: 9, with 3 showing executive sponsor depth increase during Q1. Top-20 at-risk accounts with executive sponsor review scheduled this week: 2.
trace: Executive sponsor engagement patterns show mixed signals: strengthening in closed renewals, requiring attention in at-risk accounts.

title: April expansion sourcing from health reviews climbs to 8 opportunities worth $340K
anchor: Customer health reviews generated 8 expansion opportunities totaling $340K in April.
connect: March health reviews sourced 6 opportunities with 83% sales acceptance rate.
body: Customer health reviews generated 8 expansion opportunities totaling $340K in April. March health reviews sourced 6 opportunities with 83% sales acceptance rate.
trace: Health review expansion sourcing accelerated in volume and value, building on March's high sales acceptance pattern.

title: Tech-touch GRR improves 4 points quarter over quarter to 82%
anchor: Q2 tech-touch GRR sits at 82% versus Q1's 86% across 98 accounts.
connect: High-touch GRR holds at 96% across 22 accounts representing top ARR concentration.
body: Q2 tech-touch GRR sits at 82% versus Q1's 86% across 98 accounts. High-touch GRR holds at 96% across 22 accounts representing top ARR concentration.
trace: Tech-touch tier shows retention decline while high-touch maintains premium performance, highlighting coverage model effectiveness at scale.

title: Small-business segment maintains 86% GRR with minimal expansion at 102% NRR
anchor: Q2 small-business GRR at 86% with NRR at 102% across $252K renewing book.
connect: Mid-market NRR runs 10 points higher at 112% over the same period.
body: Q2 small-business GRR at 86% with NRR at 102% across $252K renewing book. Mid-market NRR runs 10 points higher at 112% over the same period.
trace: Small-business segment shows retention challenges and minimal expansion behavior versus mid-market acceleration patterns.

title: Multi-product NRR accelerates 8 points quarter over quarter to 124%
anchor: Multi-product customer NRR jumps from Q1's 116% to Q2's 124% across 46 accounts.
connect: Single-product NRR holds steady at 102% across both quarters.
body: Multi-product customer NRR jumps from Q1's 116% to Q2's 124% across 46 accounts. Single-product NRR holds steady at 102% across both quarters.
trace: Product breadth drives compounding expansion behavior with multi-product customers showing accelerating NRR while single-product remains flat.
